name: Castro de las Labradas Loop
name_original: Villaferrueña a Sierra de las Labradas — circular por la Vía militar romana El Tesoro
description: Hike the Castro de las Labradas Loop, a moderate 8.7-mile trail exploring ancient Astur settlements and offering panoramic views in Zamora.
region_intro: Hiking around Villaferrueña offers a variety of routes through diverse landscapes. The region features riverbanks, such as those of the Eria and Órbigo, alongside areas with hills and historical sites like dolmens and castles. Trails often traverse agricultural lands and provide views of the surrounding countryside. The terrain includes gentle ascents and descents, suitable for various hiking experiences. Best hiking trails around Villaferrueña The most popular hiking route is Basilia's Balcony and Coto, a 4.9 miles (7.8 km) trail that takes 2 hours 13 minutes to complete. This moderate route features varied terrain with some elevation gain. Another top favourite among local hikers is Banks of the Eria and Órbigo Trail, a moderate 8.3 miles (13.4 km) path. This route follows the scenic riverbanks, offering flat sections and natural views. Local hikers also love the Peña Redonda and miradores del Coto and de las Suertes — circular by the Valles de Benavente from Morales de Rey, a 8.5 miles (13.7 km) trail leading through the Valles de Benavente, often completed in about 3 hours 43 minutes. Hiking around Villaferrueña is defined by river valleys, agricultural landscapes, and historical points of interest. The network offers options for easy and moderate ability levels, with 3 easy and 9 moderate routes available. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 10 hikers have used komoot to explore Villaferrueña's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are there around Villaferrueña?
A: There are &lt;strong&gt;12 hiking routes&lt;/strong&gt; around Villaferrueña listed in this guide, offering a variety of experiences. You'll find 3 easy routes and 9 moderate options, ensuring there's something for different fitness levels.

Q: Are there any easy hiking trails suitable for beginners or families in Villaferrueña?
A: Yes, Villaferrueña offers 3 easy hiking trails. These routes are generally shorter and have less elevation gain, making them ideal for beginners, families with children, or those looking for a relaxed stroll through the scenic landscapes.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on hikes around Villaferrueña?
A: The terrain around Villaferrueña is diverse, featuring riverbanks along the Eria and Órbigo, gentle hills, and agricultural lands. Many trails offer views of the surrounding countryside and may include historical sites like dolmens. Expect a mix of flat sections and mild ascents/descents.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in the Villaferrueña area?
A: Yes, there are circular routes available. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1022509543/pena-redonda-and-miradores-del-coto-and-de-las-suertes-circular-by-the-valles-de-benavente-from-morales-de-rey" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña Redonda and miradores del Coto and de las Suertes — circular by the Valles de Benavente from Morales de Rey&lt;/a&gt; is a popular moderate option that allows you to start and end in the same location.

Q: What historical sites or landmarks can I see while hiking near Villaferrueña?
A: The region is rich in history. You can explore sites like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5966274" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Dolmen de San Adrián&lt;/a&gt; or the ancient &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5453005" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castro de Las Labradas&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1305829771/route-of-the-dolmens-and-castles-of-granucillo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Route of the Dolmens and Castles of Granucillo&lt;/a&gt; specifically highlights these historical points of interest.

Q: Are there any castles to visit near the hiking trails?
A: Yes, the area around Villaferrueña features several castles. Notable examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5786385" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de los Pimentel&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5453127" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Granucillo Castle&lt;/a&gt;, offering glimpses into the region's past.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Villaferrueña?
A: Hikers often praise the diverse landscapes, from tranquil riverbanks to rolling agricultural fields. The presence of historical sites like dolmens and castles, combined with well-maintained trails, makes for an engaging experience. The komoot community has actively explored these routes, indicating their appeal.

Q: Are there any routes that follow riverbanks?
A: Absolutely. The region is characterized by its rivers. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1022487025/banks-of-the-eria-and-orbigo-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Banks of the Eria and Órbigo Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a prime example, offering scenic views along these waterways with mostly flat sections.

Q: Are there any moderate hikes for those looking for a bit more challenge?
A: Yes, the majority of trails (9 out of 12) are rated as moderate. These routes, such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1305549898/arrabalde-circular-through-the-sierra-de-las-labradas" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arrabalde — circular through the Sierra de las Labradas&lt;/a&gt;, typically involve longer distances or more significant elevation changes compared to easy trails, providing a good workout while still being accessible.

Q: Is there parking available at the trailheads?
A: While specific parking details for each trailhead are best checked on individual route pages, most hiking areas in rural regions like Villaferrueña typically offer roadside parking or small designated areas near villages or points of interest. It's advisable to plan ahead and check the starting points of your chosen route.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Villaferrueña?
A: The region's climate generally makes spring and autumn ideal for hiking, with milder temperatures and pleasant scenery. Summer can be warm, especially in open agricultural areas, while winter might bring cooler weather, though trails remain accessible for those prepared.
tips: Ruined bridge over the Ería River, on the outskirts of the town of Villaferrueña. The river scenery is a beautiful backdrop for your adventure.
Roman military road that communicates camps that surrounded the castro of Las Labradas.
Lagoon created by an ancient Roman gravel pit for the construction of the military road that passes nearby. It serves as a pond for fawning mallards.
One of dozens of prehistoric stone structures found in this area.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to hike the Castro de las Labradas Loop?
faqs.answer: The Castro de las Labradas Loop is approximately 14 kilometers (8.7 miles) long and generally takes around 3 hours and 57 minutes to complete. This duration is for the full loop starting from Villaferrueña.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of this hiking trail?
faqs.answer: The trail is rated as moderate. While the paths are mostly accessible, it involves an elevation gain of about 268 meters and requires a good level of fitness and sure-footedness. It's suitable for hikers with some experience.

faqs.question: Where can I park for the Castro de las Labradas Loop trailhead?
faqs.answer: The loop can be accessed from Villaferrueña. Parking is typically available in or near the villages surrounding the Sierra de las Labradas, such as Villaferrueña or Arrabalde. It's advisable to check local signage upon arrival for designated parking areas.

faqs.question: Is the Castro de las Labradas Loop dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, the trail is generally dog-friendly. However, it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially when passing through agricultural areas or near wildlife, and to respect local regulations.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: The Castro de las Labradas Loop is suitable for hiking in all seasons. Each season offers a different experience, from lush greenery in spring to clear views in autumn. Always check the local weather forecast before you go.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee for the Castro de las Labradas Loop?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to hike the Castro de las Labradas Loop. The trail is freely accessible to the public.

faqs.question: What historical sites and natural features can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: The trail is rich in history and natural beauty. You'll encounter the Old Bridge of Villaferrueña, the ancient Roman Military Road El Tesoro, and the serene Laguna del Tesoro. A major highlight is the Prehistoric Earth Structure, which is part of one of the largest fortified Iron Age Astur settlements. You can also see remnants of extensive walls, access gates, and outlines of ancient dwellings.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Castro de las Labradas Loop?
faqs.answer: The terrain consists of a combination of tracks and accessible trails. You'll traverse areas with pine, holm oak, and oak forests. The route also utilizes natural defenses of rocky massifs, contributing to the varied and interesting landscape.

faqs.question: Are there any notable viewpoints along the trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, as the trail ascends to approximately 1,000 meters, about 200 meters above the Eria river plain, it offers impressive panoramic views. On clear days, you can see Monte Teleno, the Aquilianos, and parts of the Cantabrian mountain range.

faqs.question: Is the Castro de las Labradas Loop suitable for families with children?
faqs.answer: Given its moderate difficulty and length, it might be challenging for very young children. However, older children with some hiking experience and good fitness levels could enjoy it, especially with the historical points of interest. Consider shorter sections if hiking with younger family members.

faqs.question: Are there amenities or accommodation options near the trail?
faqs.answer: While the trail itself is natural, the nearby village of Arrabalde hosts an archaeological classroom that provides further context about the site. For amenities like cafes, pubs, or accommodation, you would typically find options in the surrounding municipalities like Arrabalde or Villaferrueña.
